source_pr.title: *: avoid flaky test with high load scenario
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: None

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

When `TestMemoryControlWithUpdate` runs in high load environment, it's not enough to wait [1 second](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/c550aae6a125e20c2aaa8d4f11a2f090bc8b2b50/pkg/statistics/handle/cache/internal/lfu/lfu_cache_test.go#L313), we set it to 5 seconds as a quick workaround.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- NA

Documentation

- NA

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note

```

symptoms: *: avoid flaky test with high load scenario
failure_signature: *: avoid flaky test with high load scenario
root_cause_explanation: The test was timing-sensitive: it slept for a fixed 1s and then asserted `lfu.Cost()==0`, but under high load the background cleanup/eviction could take longer and the assertion would intermittently fail. The fix removes the brittle `time.Sleep` and uses `require.Eventually` with a reasonable timeout/interval so the test waits deterministically for the expected state.
fix_pattern: Replace fixed sleep + immediate assertion with `require.Eventually` (longer timeout) to avoid timing-dependent flakiness under load.

evidence: evidence: *: avoid flaky test with high load scenario
